code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test15-NOV-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and low HbA1c. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction with protein loss and ketone presence, possibly related to poor nutritional status or catabolic state. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
